messages.content:
You are a GDPR compliance analyst. When given a document excerpt, evaluate it step by step against each GDPR requirement. For each step, quote the relevant text if found, or state it is missing. End with a structured verdict listing all violations. Always follow this exact format:

Step 1 — Lawful basis (Art. 6): ...
Step 2 — Special category data (Art. 9): ...
Step 3 — Purpose limitation (Art. 5): ...
Step 4 — Data minimization (Art. 5): ...
Step 5 — Retention period (Art. 5): ...
Step 6 — Data subject rights (Art. 15-21): ...
Step 7 — Third-party disclosure (Art. 28): ...
Step 8 — International transfers (Art. 46): ...
Step 9 — DPO appointment (Art. 37): ...
Step 10 — Breach notification (Art. 33): ...

VERDICT: [Compliant / Non-Compliant / Partially Compliant]
VIOLATIONS: [list each article breached]
RECOMMENDATIONS: [one line per fix needed]

Assess the following charity donation platform privacy notice for GDPR compliance:

"GiveNow processes your name, email, and payment details to process your donation and send you a receipt. We process this data on the basis of contract performance. With your separate consent, we will send you updates about the causes you support. Donation records are retained for 7 years for Gift Aid and accounting purposes. You may access, correct, or delete your data by emailing privacy@givenow.org. We use Stripe for payment processing under a signed DPA. All data is stored in EU data centres. Our DPO is at dpo@givenow.org. We notify the ICO within 72 hours of a breach."

Step 1 — Lawful basis (Art. 6): [PASS] Contract performance (Art. 6(1)(b)) for donation processing. Separate consent (Art. 6(1)(a)) for marketing updates. Dual basis correctly applied.

Step 2 — Special category data (Art. 9): [PARTIAL] Donation to certain charities (religious, political, health-related) can infer special category data. This risk is not acknowledged.

Step 3 — Purpose limitation (Art. 5): [PASS] Donation processing, receipt sending, Gift Aid, accounting, and optional updates are each separately stated.

Step 4 — Data minimization (Art. 5): [PASS] Name, email, and payment details are standard and proportionate.

Step 5 — Retention period (Art. 5): [PASS] 7-year retention stated and grounded in Gift Aid and accounting legal obligations.

Step 6 — Data subject rights (Art. 15-21): [PARTIAL] Access (Art. 15), rectification (Art. 16), and erasure (Art. 17) mentioned. Rights to portability (Art. 20), restriction (Art. 18), and objection (Art. 21) not stated.

Step 7 — Third-party disclosure (Art. 28): [PASS] Stripe named as payment processor with signed DPA.

Step 8 — International transfers (Art. 46): [PASS] EU data centres confirmed. No transfer concerns.

Step 9 — DPO appointment (Art. 37): [PASS] DPO contact at dpo@givenow.org disclosed.

Step 10 — Breach notification (Art. 33): [PASS] 72-hour ICO notification stated.

VERDICT: Partially Compliant
VIOLATIONS: Art. 15-21 (portability, restriction, objection rights absent)
RECOMMENDATIONS:
- Add rights to portability, restriction, and objection with contact instructions
- Consider acknowledging that donations to certain causes may imply sensitive beliefs and confirm this is handled appropriately
